perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to either Party, all entities which, directly or indirectly, are controlled by, control or are under common control with such Party. For purposes of this Agreement, the word "control" shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, including through ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting shares or interest of an entity; provided, however, with respect to CAPSUGEL, the term "Affiliate" shall be limited to entities who directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries are controlled by the parent of CAPSUGEL's direct parent entity and with respect to CARDAX the term "Affiliate" shall not include Cardax Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to either Party, all entities which, directly or indirectly, are affiliated with, works in conjunction with, or has a strategic partnership with such Party. For purposes of this Agreement, the word "control" shall broadly encompass any form of association, collaboration, or alliance, irrespective of ownership percentage, that allows one party to exert influence over the other's operations or decisions; provided, however, with respect to CAPSUGEL, the term "Affiliate" shall be limited to entities who directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries are controlled by the parent of CAPSUGEL's direct parent entity and with respect to CARDAX the term "Affiliate" shall not include Cardax Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of "Affiliate" to include entities with strategic partnerships or loose affiliations, as well as changing the definition of "control" to loosely encompass any form of influence, rather than just entities under majority control (more than 50% ownership). This contradicts other contract sections that rely on the original, stricter definition to determine rights and obligations. By creating a broader, less precise definition, the modification introduces legal uncertainty and could lead to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will own all of its inventions and other Intellectual Property Rights made under this Agreement, including any patents, patent applications and other Intellectual Property Rights related to such inventions, if any, made solely by its employees or independent contractors or employees or independent contractors of its Affiliates, unless otherwise expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The modified text broadens the definition of 'Affiliate' and 'control' to include strategic partnerships and any form of influence, irrespective of ownership percentage. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants intellectual property rights based on inventions made by employees or contractors of 'Affiliates.' Since the original definition of Affiliate focused on entities under majority control (more than 50% ownership), the changed text introduces uncertainty. Now, inventions by strategic partners (who are not necessarily under majority control) could potentially fall under the definition of Affiliate, thus impacting ownership of intellectual property. This is a clear contradiction because the original text implies a narrower scope of affiliation for IP rights, while the modified text creates a broader, ambiguous scope. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction,' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Royalty Payments. CAPSUGEL shall pay to CARDAX a royalty equal to [***] of the Adjusted Net Sales ("Royalty Payment") within [***] after the end of [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Royalty Payments. CAPSUGEL shall pay to CARDAX a royalty equal to [***] of the Adjusted Net Sales ("Royalty Payment") within [***] after the end of [***], provided, however, that if the Marketer's payments to CAPSUGEL are delayed for any reason, the Royalty Payment shall be due within 180 days of the original end of [***].
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by introducing a new condition that delays the payment of royalties to CARDAX based on the Marketer's payments to CAPSUGEL. This contradicts with the previously established payment deadline of within [***] after the end of [***] in Section 5.1. This introduces legal uncertainty and could lead to disputes over payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, the Exhibits hereto contain the complete agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. All previous and collateral agreements, representations, warranties, promises and conditions relating to the subject matter of this Agreement are superseded by this Agreement. This Agreement may only be amended by a written instrument duly executed by the Parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5.1 specifies a clear deadline for royalty payments. The changed text introduces a conditional delay to this deadline, based on a third party's payment behavior. This directly contradicts the 'complete agreement' clause, which states that the agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties and can only be amended by a written instrument duly executed by the parties. The added condition effectively amends the payment terms without such a written amendment, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Regulatory Contacts. [***] CAPSUGEL shall notify CARDAX immediately, and in no event, no later than three (3) business day(s), after it receives any contact or communication from any governmental or regulatory authority, including without limitation the FDA, that in any way relates to or may have an impact on a Product or the CAPSUGEL Development Activities.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Regulatory Contacts. [***] CAPSUGEL shall notify CARDAX as soon as reasonably practicable, but in no event, no later than ten (10) business day(s), after it receives any contact or communication from any governmental or regulatory authority, including without limitation the FDA, that in any way relates to or may have an impact on a Product or the CAPSUGEL Development Activities.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the original notification timeline from immediately, and no later than three (3) business days, to ten (10) business days, after CAPSUGEL receives communication from regulatory authorities. This contradicts the sense of immediacy intended in the original version and introduces a potential delay in critical information sharing. Which may result in disputes over the promptness of CAPSUGEL informing CARDAX about critical regulatory communication.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CAPSUGEL shall provide prompt written notice to CARDAX of the occurrence of, and the results of any regulatory notices including inspections as referenced in this Section 6 relating to the manufacture of Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text required notification of regulatory contact within three business days. The changed text extends this to ten business days. The contradicted text requires 'prompt written notice.' Extending the notification period to ten days directly contradicts the 'prompt' notification requirement, creating a strong inconsistency as defined in the 'Inconsistencies' definition of perturbation types.
